metadata.name: roundfi_core
metadata.description: RoundFi core — ROSCA pool state machine, escrow, and solidarity vault

instructions.name: cancel_new_authority
docs: Authority rotation step 2/3 (optional) — abort a pending
authority proposal before its eta. Authority-only.

instructions.name: cancel_new_fee_bps_yield
docs: Fee timelock step 2/3 (optional) — abort a pending fee_bps_yield
proposal before its eta. Authority-only.

instructions.name: cancel_new_treasury
docs: Treasury rotation step 2/3 (optional) — abort a pending
proposal before its eta. Authority-only.

instructions.name: cancel_pending_listing
docs: Seller-only abort of a `Pending` escape-valve listing.
Closes the listing PDA, refunds rent to seller, frees the
slot for a fresh commit. Adevar Labs SEV-015 fix — without
this ix, a seller who commits but never reveals locks the
slot indefinitely (PDA seeds tie the slot to the listing).

accounts.name: listing
docs: The Pending listing being cancelled. Closes back to
`seller_wallet` so rent is reclaimed.

instructions.name: close_member
docs: SEV-039: reclaim a finalized pool's per-member rent by closing one
Member PDA after the pool is Closed (decrements the live-member count
so `close_pool_vaults` knows when it's safe to close the Pool PDA).
See `instructions::close_member`.

accounts.name: config
docs: Protocol singleton. Mutable so the handler can decrement the
running `committed_protocol_tvl_usdc` total when the pool's
max-flow contribution leaves the active set (TVL caps —
items 4.2 + 4.3 of `MAINNET_READINESS.md`).

accounts.name: authority
docs: Authority must match the pool's creator OR the protocol authority.

instructions.name: close_pool_vaults
docs: SEV-039 (final step): drain the four vaults' residual USDC to the
protocol treasury, close the four vault ATAs, and close the Pool PDA —
reclaiming all of that rent. Requires every Member PDA closed first
(`pool.members_joined == 0`). Ceremony: `close_pool` → `close_member`
× N → `close_pool_vaults`. See `instructions::close_pool_vaults`.

accounts.name: rent_recipient
docs: Receives the rent freed by closing the 4 vault ATAs + the Pool PDA.

accounts.name: pool
docs: Closed via Anchor `close = rent_recipient` AFTER the handler drains +
closes the vaults (the handler still needs the live Pool PDA to sign the
`pool_usdc_vault` transfer/close — it is the vault's token authority).

accounts.name: treasury_usdc
docs: Drain destination — pinned to `config.treasury` (the treasury USDC token
account set at `initialize_protocol`).

instructions.name: commit_new_authority
docs: Authority rotation step 3/3 — commit a pending authority
proposal once its eta has passed. Anyone can crank (timelock
is the gate). After commit, the new authority controls every
authority-gated ix.

accounts.name: caller
docs: Anyone can crank — no signer/authority constraint. Gate is the
timelock + the prior `propose_new_authority` signed by the
then-current authority.

instructions.name: commit_new_fee_bps_yield
docs: Fee timelock step 3/3 — commit a pending fee_bps_yield proposal
once its eta has passed. Anyone can crank (timelock is the gate).

accounts.name: caller
docs: Adevar Labs SEV-037 — added for consistency with the other
`commit_new_*` ixs (CommitNewTreasury, CommitNewAuthority).
The crank is still permissionless (no authority gate), but a
signer is required so the tx has an explicit fee-payer and
the account-list shape matches the rest of the timelock
commit family. Same convention these other handlers use.

instructions.name: commit_new_treasury
docs: Treasury rotation step 3/3 — commit a pending proposal once
its eta has passed. Anyone can crank (timelock is the gate).

accounts.name: caller
docs: Anyone can crank — no signer/authority constraint.

accounts.name: identity_record
docs: to signal "no identity linked" (Anchor Option<Account> convention).

accounts.name: escrow_vault_authority
docs: USDC ATA gets created in `init_pool_vaults`.

accounts.name: caller
docs: Anyone can crank this; the pool PDA authorizes the transfer.

accounts.name: yield_vault
docs: The adapter-side vault. Authority is adapter-controlled; we never
verify its layout, only read its `amount` field before/after CPI.

accounts.name: seller_wallet
docs: listing rent refund. Must match `listing.seller`.

accounts.name: nft_asset
docs: `old_member.nft_asset` so the buyer can't substitute someone
else's NFT mid-transfer.

accounts.name: position_authority
docs: of the position NFT (set in join_pool). Signs the asset transfer
CPIs via PDA seeds.

instructions.name: escape_valve_list_commit
docs: Commit-reveal step 1/2 (#232). Creates a listing in `Pending`
status with only a hash of `(price || salt)` stored on chain —
hides the price from searchers monitoring escape-valve flow.

instructions.name: escape_valve_list_reveal
docs: Commit-reveal step 2/2 (#232). Validates the (price, salt)
pair against the stored hash, transitions the listing to
`Active`, and arms a `REVEAL_COOLDOWN_SECS` window before
the listing becomes buyable.

accounts.name: treasury_usdc
docs: Protocol treasury — pinned to config.treasury.
`config.treasury` is set at `initialize_protocol` time to the
treasury TOKEN ACCOUNT's pubkey (see `initialize_protocol.rs:75`),
not the wallet that owns it. The check is therefore `key() ==`,
not `owner ==`.

accounts.name: yield_vault
docs: pending yield. Intentionally `UncheckedAccount` — we do NOT
enforce `owner == pool.yield_adapter` here for two reasons:
1. The adapter program ID is enforced separately via
`yield_adapter_program.key() == pool.yield_adapter`
inside `invoke_adapter` (see `cpi/yield_adapter.rs`).
2. The vault's authority topology is adapter-defined (e.g.
Kamino uses a strategy PDA as authority; the mock uses
its own state PDA). Hard-coding owner here would require
a per-adapter switch and re-introduce the trust we've
already moved into the program-id pin.
What protects us instead is the **delta-balance** mitigation:
we snapshot `yield_vault_before` + `vault_before`, run the CPI,
then assert `yield_vault_drop <= realized + 1` (catches an
adapter that drains principal as if it were yield) and the
caller-provided `min_realized_usdc` slippage guard (catches an
adapter that under-reports yield to pocket the difference).
Together those make `yield_vault.owner` immaterial — the
authoritative number is the post-CPI delta on `pool_usdc_vault`,
which IS owner-checked (it's an Anchor `TokenAccount`).

instructions.name: init_pool_vaults
docs: Second half of pool creation — initializes the four USDC vault
ATAs (pool/escrow/solidarity/yield) via sequential CPIs to the
SPL Associated Token Program. Must be called after `create_pool`
and before any `join_pool`. Idempotent.

accounts.name: config
docs: Protocol singleton. Mutable so the handler can update the
running `committed_protocol_tvl_usdc` total for the new pool's
max-flow contribution (TVL cap enforcement — items 4.2 + 4.3
of `MAINNET_READINESS.md`).

accounts.name: pool
docs: Pool created by `create_pool`. Bumps for the three vault
authority PDAs are read from here so this ix doesn't have to
re-derive them at compute cost.

accounts.name: pool_usdc_vault
docs: CPI inside the handler.

accounts.name: treasury
docs: USDC token account receiving protocol fees; must be on the configured mint.

instructions.name: lock_approved_yield_adapter
docs: One-way kill switch — once called, `approved_yield_adapter`
cannot be changed (mirrors `lock_treasury` for the adapter
allowlist). Authority-only, idempotent. Governance hardening
for the canary-rampup → mainnet-pinned transition.

instructions.name: lock_reputation_program
docs: Confirm on-chain that `reputation_program` was initialised to
a real program (refuses to lock when it equals `Pubkey::default()`).
Authority-only, idempotent, one-way. Mainnet operators call this
immediately after `initialize_protocol` so the live deployment
can't silently run in the no-reputation skip-path mode.
Partner review MEDIUM #2 (2026-06-12).

instructions.name: lock_treasury
docs: One-way kill switch — once called, treasury cannot be rotated
again (existing pending proposals still commit). Authority-only.

instructions.name: migrate_protocol_config
docs: One-shot rescue ix for `ProtocolConfig` accounts created under an
earlier (smaller) struct layout. Reallocates to the current
`ProtocolConfig::SIZE` with zero-init tail bytes + pays the rent
delta + writes `DEFAULT_LP_SHARE_BPS` (the only field where zero
would be the wrong default). Idempotent: a no-op if the account
is already at the target size. Authority is the on-chain
authority slot (validated via raw bytes — the struct doesn't
deserialize yet). Devnet-rescue scope; removed in a follow-up
wave once devnet is on the current layout. See the module
docstring for the field-by-field justification.

accounts.name: config
docs: on-chain account is at the OLD layout (smaller than the current
`ProtocolConfig::SIZE`). We validate manually: discriminator,
PDA seeds, owner == program, and authority bytes.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Must match the authority slot in the existing config bytes.

instructions.name: ping
docs: Dev-only smoke instruction; retained until Step 10 deprecates it.

instructions.name: propose_new_authority
docs: Authority rotation step 1/3 — stage a new protocol authority
behind a `TREASURY_TIMELOCK_SECS` (7d) window. Mirrors the
treasury propose/commit pattern (#122). At mainnet Squads
ceremony, this is the bootstrap from deployer → multisig
vault PDA. Reverts if another proposal is already pending.
Authority-only.

instructions.name: propose_new_fee_bps_yield
docs: Fee timelock step 1/3 — propose a new `fee_bps_yield` value.
Stages on `pending_fee_bps_yield` behind a 1-day public window
(`FEE_BPS_YIELD_TIMELOCK_SECS`). Adevar Labs SEV-024 follow-up:
SEV-024 capped fee_bps_yield at 30%, this PR adds the timelock
gate so users can detect a fee change and opt out via the
escape valve before it takes effect. Authority-only.

instructions.name: propose_new_treasury
docs: Treasury rotation step 1/3 — stage a new treasury behind a
`TREASURY_TIMELOCK_SECS` (7d) window. Reverts if locked or
another proposal is already pending. Authority-only.

accounts.name: usdc_mint
docs: USDC mint, pinned to `config.usdc_mint`. Used by the
`new_treasury` constraint to enforce it's an ATA on the
correct mint.

accounts.name: new_treasury
docs: The proposed new treasury — must be an existing TokenAccount
on `config.usdc_mint`. Anchor validates the layout + mint at
account-validation time, so a typo / wrong-mint / wallet
pubkey rejects HERE rather than after a 7-day wait.
Adevar Labs SEV-006 — previously a raw Pubkey in args.

accounts.name: defaulted_member_wallet
docs: Validated by constraint against `member.wallet`; does NOT sign.

instructions.name: skip_defaulted_payout
docs: Permissionless cycle advance for a slot whose contemplated member
defaulted pre-contemplation (otherwise the pool locks — claim_payout
requires `!defaulted` but only it advances the cycle). No payout; the
forfeited pot stays in the float. See `instructions::skip_defaulted_payout`.

accounts.name: caller
docs: Permissionless crank — anyone can unstick the pool. Pays only tx fees.

errors.name: InsufficientStake
errors.msg: Stake below required amount for this reputation level

errors.name: PoolFull
errors.msg: Pool has reached members_target

errors.name: PoolNotForming
errors.msg: Pool is not in Forming state

errors.name: PoolNotActive
errors.msg: Pool is not in Active state

errors.name: PoolClosed
errors.msg: Pool is Completed or Liquidated

errors.name: AlreadyJoined
errors.msg: Wallet already has a Member account for this pool

errors.name: NotAMember
errors.msg: Caller is not a member of this pool

errors.name: WrongCycle
errors.msg: Cycle index does not match pool.current_cycle

errors.name: CycleNotReady
errors.msg: next_cycle_at not yet reached

errors.name: AlreadyContributed
errors.msg: Member already contributed for this cycle

errors.name: NotYourPayoutSlot
errors.msg: Caller is not the slot owner for this cycle

errors.name: EscrowLocked
errors.msg: Escrow release schedule not yet vested

errors.name: EscrowNothingToRelease
errors.msg: Nothing to release from escrow

errors.name: DefaultedMember
errors.msg: Member has defaulted

errors.name: SeedDrawShortfall
errors.msg: Seed Draw invariant would be violated

errors.name: SolidarityOverflow
errors.msg: Solidarity accounting overflow

errors.name: InvalidYieldAdapter
errors.msg: Yield adapter program mismatch or not whitelisted

errors.name: YieldAdapterMismatch
errors.msg: Pool's yield_adapter does not match passed program

errors.name: WaterfallUnderflow
errors.msg: Yield waterfall underflow or reordering detected

errors.name: AttestationSchemaMismatch
errors.msg: Attestation schema does not match expected

errors.name: ReputationUnderflow
errors.msg: Reputation score underflow

errors.name: InvalidReputationLevel
errors.msg: Reputation level must be 1, 2, or 3

errors.name: MathOverflow
errors.msg: Arithmetic overflow

errors.name: Unauthorized
errors.msg: Caller is not authorized

errors.name: ProtocolPaused
errors.msg: Protocol is paused

errors.name: InvalidMint
errors.msg: Token mint does not match the expected USDC mint

errors.name: InvalidNftAsset
errors.msg: NFT asset does not match Member.nft_asset

errors.name: EscapeValveNotListed
errors.msg: Position is not listed on the escape valve

errors.name: EscapeValvePriceMismatch
errors.msg: Escape valve price mismatch

errors.name: SlotTaken
errors.msg: Slot is already taken

errors.name: InvalidSlot
errors.msg: Slot index out of range

errors.name: InvalidBps
errors.msg: Bps value must be <= 10_000 (or <= 50_000 for guarantee fund)

errors.name: InvalidMembersTarget
errors.msg: members_target must be in 1..=MAX_MEMBERS

errors.name: InvalidCycleDuration
errors.msg: cycle_duration below minimum

errors.name: InvalidAmount
errors.msg: Amount must be non-zero

errors.name: InvalidPoolParams
errors.msg: Pool parameters are inconsistent

errors.name: MetadataUriTooLong
errors.msg: Metadata URI exceeds MAX_URI_LEN

errors.name: MetadataUriInvalidScheme
errors.msg: Metadata URI scheme not allowed (must be https://, ipfs://, or ar://)

errors.name: GracePeriodNotElapsed
errors.msg: Grace period not yet elapsed — default cannot be settled

errors.name: MemberNotBehind
errors.msg: Member is current on contributions — default not applicable

errors.name: DebtCollateralViolation
errors.msg: Debt/collateral invariant would be violated: D/D_init > C/C_init

errors.name: YieldAdapterBalanceMismatch
errors.msg: Yield adapter returned a nonsensical balance delta

errors.name: WaterfallNotConserved
errors.msg: Waterfall bucket computation did not conserve total

errors.name: InvalidListingPrice
errors.msg: Listing price must be non-zero

errors.name: ListingNotActive
errors.msg: Listing is not active

errors.name: BuyerAlreadyMember
errors.msg: Buyer already holds a Member account for this pool

errors.name: PoolNotCompleted
errors.msg: Pool is not in Completed state

errors.name: ImmutableConfigField
errors.msg: Protocol config field is immutable after initialization

errors.name: TreasuryLocked
errors.msg: Treasury is permanently locked — `lock_treasury()` was called

errors.name: NoPendingTreasuryChange
errors.msg: No pending treasury rotation to commit or cancel

errors.name: TreasuryTimelockActive
errors.msg: Treasury rotation timelock has not yet elapsed

errors.name: TreasuryProposalAlreadyPending
errors.msg: A treasury rotation is already pending — cancel it first

errors.name: AssetTransferIncomplete
errors.msg: Post-CPI verification: asset owner is not the buyer after transfer

errors.name: AssetNotRefrozen
errors.msg: Post-CPI verification: asset is not frozen after re-freeze step

errors.name: HarvestSlippageExceeded
errors.msg: Harvest realized yield is below the caller's slippage threshold

errors.name: OutstandingDefaults
errors.msg: Pool has outstanding defaults — cannot close

errors.name: YieldAdapterNotConfigured
errors.msg: Yield adapter not configured for this pool

errors.name: ReputationCpiFailed
errors.msg: roundfi-reputation CPI failed — the attestation was rejected by the target program

errors.name: ReputationProgramMismatch
errors.msg: Passed reputation program does not match config.reputation_program

errors.name: ReputationLevelMismatch
errors.msg: Asserted reputation_level does not match the on-chain ReputationProfile

errors.name: PoolTvlCapExceeded
errors.msg: Pool max committed flow (credit_amount × cycles_total) exceeds config.max_pool_tvl_usdc

errors.name: ProtocolTvlCapExceeded
errors.msg: Protocol-wide committed TVL would exceed config.max_protocol_tvl_usdc

errors.name: AdapterAllowlistLocked
errors.msg: approved_yield_adapter is permanently locked — `lock_approved_yield_adapter()` was called

errors.name: CommitRevealRequired
errors.msg: Legacy single-step escape_valve_list disabled by config.commit_reveal_required — use the commit + reveal flow

errors.name: ListingNotPending
errors.msg: Listing is not in Pending status (reveal only valid on a committed-but-not-yet-revealed listing)

errors.name: InvalidCommitHash
errors.msg: Reveal price/salt does not match the stored commit_hash

errors.name: ListingNotBuyableYet
errors.msg: Listing is not yet buyable — wait for REVEAL_COOLDOWN_SECS to elapse past buyable_after

errors.name: NoPendingAuthorityChange
errors.msg: No pending authority rotation to commit or cancel

errors.name: AuthorityTimelockActive
errors.msg: Authority rotation timelock has not yet elapsed

errors.name: AuthorityProposalAlreadyPending
errors.msg: An authority rotation is already pending — cancel it first

errors.name: VaultsAlreadyInitialized
errors.msg: Pool vaults are already initialized — init_pool_vaults is one-shot

errors.name: SaltMustBeNonZero
errors.msg: Commit-reveal salt cannot be zero — use a cryptographically random u64

errors.name: PoolNotViable
errors.msg: Pool parameters are inviable: members × installment × (1 − solidarity − escrow) < credit (would fail cycle-0 Seed Draw guard)

errors.name: NoPendingFeeBpsYieldChange
errors.msg: No pending fee_bps_yield change to commit or cancel

errors.name: FeeBpsYieldTimelockActive
errors.msg: fee_bps_yield timelock has not yet elapsed

errors.name: FeeBpsYieldProposalAlreadyPending
errors.msg: A fee_bps_yield change is already pending — cancel it first

errors.name: DirectFeeBpsYieldMutationDisabled
errors.msg: Direct mutation of fee_bps_yield is disabled — use propose/cancel/commit timelock flow

errors.name: SlotNotDefaulted
errors.msg: Slot's contemplated member is not defaulted — use claim_payout, not skip_defaulted_payout

errors.name: PoolNotClosed
errors.msg: Pool is not in Closed state — call close_pool first before close_member

errors.name: MembersStillOpen
errors.msg: Pool still has open Member PDAs — close them all via close_member before close_pool_vaults

fields.name: cycle
docs: Must equal `pool.current_cycle` AND `member.slot_index`.

fields.name: cycle
docs: Must equal `pool.current_cycle` AND `member.contributions_paid` —
enforces ordered contributions (no skipping / no retroactive pay).

fields.name: amount
docs: Amount of USDC (base units) to move from pool vault to yield vault.
The adapter may deposit less than requested (e.g. due to caps) —
we book the actual post-CPI delta.

fields.name: price_usdc
docs: Must equal `listing.price_usdc` — buyer commits to the price
they saw off-chain. Rejects if the seller changed price between
view and buy.

fields.name: commit_hash
docs: `SHA-256(price_usdc.to_le_bytes() || salt.to_le_bytes())`.
32 bytes. Hash format is fixed — the reveal handler
recomputes it byte-for-byte from the (price, salt) args.
**Salt entropy requirement (Adevar Labs SEV-013):** the `salt`
half of the pre-image MUST be cryptographically random. SDK
helpers should use `crypto.randomBytes(8)` and BigInt-decode.
`salt = 0` or predictable patterns (timestamps, slot numbers,
counters) let a searcher brute-force the commit_hash by
enumerating prices in the expected range, breaking the
commit-reveal privacy property.
The reveal handler rejects `salt = 0` outright with
`SaltMustBeNonZero` as a minimal trivially-broken-case guard.
Beyond that, salt-quality enforcement is the SDK's
responsibility — we cannot verify entropy from a single
64-bit observation on chain.

types.name: EscapeValveListing
docs: Escape-valve listing for a specific slot. PDA: `[b"listing", pool, slot_index]`.
Two creation paths:
1. **Legacy single-step** — `escape_valve_list(price)` creates the
listing directly in `Active` status. `commit_hash = [0;32]`,
`buyable_after = listed_at` (immediately buyable). Devnet UX
default; mainnet-gated by `config.commit_reveal_required`.
2. **Commit-reveal** (#232 MEV mitigation) —
`escape_valve_list_commit(commit_hash)` creates the listing in
`Pending` status with `price_usdc = 0` and the hash of
`(price || salt)` stored. The seller later calls
`escape_valve_list_reveal(price, salt)` to transition
`Pending → Active`; reveal sets `buyable_after = now +
REVEAL_COOLDOWN_SECS`, giving the legitimate buyer a fixed
window to land their buy tx before the now-public price can be
sniped by a searcher.
Consumed/closed by `escape_valve_buy` (which also enforces
`now >= buyable_after`).

fields.name: commit_hash
docs: SHA-256(price.to_le_bytes() || salt.to_le_bytes()) for the
commit-reveal path. `[0u8; 32]` for legacy single-step listings
(sentinel — anything that *could* be a valid hash is fine here
because the legacy path never enters the reveal handler).

fields.name: buyable_after
docs: UNIX timestamp at which the listing becomes buyable.
- Legacy single-step: equals `listed_at` (no cooldown).
- Commit-reveal: equals `reveal_ts + REVEAL_COOLDOWN_SECS`.
`escape_valve_buy` reverts with `ListingNotBuyableYet` while
`now < buyable_after`.

fields.name: lp_share_bps
docs: **DEPRECATED — ignored at the handler level (Adevar Labs SEV-003 fix).**
Previously controlled the LP/participant split in the yield
waterfall. Was caller-controlled and unbound by any authoritative
policy — a permissionless cranker could rotate the split between
0% and 100% per call, breaking the contract with LP subscribers
(Anjos de Liquidez). The auditor flagged this as High severity.
The handler now reads `config.lp_share_bps` and IGNORES whatever
value the caller passes here. The field is retained in the args
struct for SDK back-compat — old SDKs continue to encode a u16
at this offset; the handler silently overrides it. A future SDK
version drops this field entirely (tracked as a Fase 3 cleanup).
Authoritative policy now lives at `ProtocolConfig.lp_share_bps`,
mutable via `update_protocol_config { new_lp_share_bps }`.

fields.name: min_realized_usdc
docs: Slippage guard: minimum realized USDC the caller is willing to
accept on this harvest. If the adapter returns less, the tx
reverts with `HarvestSlippageExceeded`. Pass `0` to disable
(back-compat default — caller takes whatever the adapter
delivers, including zero).
The crank computes this off-chain as
`expected_yield × (1 − tolerance_bps / 10_000)`
where `expected_yield = principal × adapter_apy × elapsed_secs / YEAR_SECS`.
Without this guard, a malicious or buggy adapter could return
e.g. 1 lamport when ~$50 USDC was due, the waterfall would
silently take a 20% fee on dust, and the residual yield
stays inside the adapter's own vault.

fields.name: guarantee_fund_bps
docs: Guarantee Fund fill target, bps of protocol yield. May exceed 10_000 (default 15_000 = 150%).

fields.name: slot_index
docs: Slot to occupy (0..members_target). Client picks the first free slot.

fields.name: reputation_level
docs: Asserted reputation level 1..=3 — the caller's expectation. The
handler derives the trusted level from the on-chain
`ReputationProfile` PDA owned by `config.reputation_program` and
rejects with `ReputationLevelMismatch` if the assertion drifts.
Closes the Step-4d audit gap (was: trusted client input).

fields.name: metadata_uri
docs: Position NFT metadata URI. Max MAX_URI_LEN bytes; scheme must be
`https://`, `ipfs://`, or `ar://` (validated below).

types.name: Member
docs: Membership record. PDA seeds: `[b"member", pool, wallet]`.

fields.name: stake_deposited_initial
docs: Stake at the moment of join_pool. Never mutated by contributions or
escrow releases — used as `C_initial` in the D/C invariant.

fields.name: total_escrow_deposited
docs: Cumulative escrow deposited over the pool life (monotonic increments
during contribute). Never decreased by release_escrow or seizure —
the "escrow half" of `C_initial`.

fields.name: last_transferred_at
docs: Timestamp the member was last re-anchored via escape_valve_buy.
0 = original buyer at join_pool.

types.name: Pool
docs: A ROSCA pool. PDA seeds: `[b"pool", authority, seed_id_le]`.

fields.name: guarantee_fund_balance
docs: Running tally of USDC currently held in the Guarantee Fund.
Replenished in step 2 of the yield waterfall (after the protocol
fee is taken on gross). Debited on default shortfall (v2).

fields.name: total_protocol_fee_accrued
docs: Cumulative protocol fee transferred to treasury over pool life.
GF top-up cap = `guarantee_fund_bps * total_protocol_fee_accrued / 10_000`.

fields.name: yield_principal_deposited
docs: Principal currently deposited in the yield adapter (tracked locally,
never trusted from adapter).

fields.name: defaulted_members
docs: Count of members that have been settled as defaulted.

fields.name: lp_distribution_balance
docs: Earmark of the LP / Anjos de Liquidez slice from step 3 of the
yield waterfall. Funds remain in `pool_usdc_vault` (logical
accounting like the Guarantee Fund) until LP withdrawal pathway
ships in M3. Don't conflate with `solidarity_balance`, which is
funded ONLY from the 1% das parcelas in `contribute()`.

fields.name: slots_bitmap
docs: Bitmap over MAX_MEMBERS=64 slots. Bit set ⇒ slot taken.

fields.name: vaults_initialized
docs: `true` once `init_pool_vaults` has run successfully. Gates the
TVL counter increment + ATA creation so a repeated call (retry,
race between interfaces, hostile authority griefing canary
rampup) cannot double-count `config.committed_protocol_tvl_usdc`.
Before this flag, the `create_idempotent` ATAs were no-op on
second call but the TVL counter increment was unconditional —
hostile authority could inflate the global counter to DoS the
`max_protocol_tvl_usdc` cap.

types.name: ProtocolConfig
docs: Protocol-wide singleton configuration. PDA seeds: `[b"config"]`.

fields.name: treasury_locked
docs: One-way kill switch. Once `true`, `treasury` is permanently
frozen — `propose_new_treasury` rejects further proposals.
Authority can call `lock_treasury()` after deployment when
confident the treasury wallet is final, getting full
immutability ON TOP of the time-lock.

fields.name: pending_treasury
docs: Pending treasury rotation. `Pubkey::default()` (all-zero) when
no rotation is queued. Set by `propose_new_treasury`, cleared
by `cancel_new_treasury` or finalized by `commit_new_treasury`.

fields.name: pending_treasury_eta
docs: Earliest unix-ts at which `commit_new_treasury` may execute.
`0` when no rotation is pending. Equals `now + TREASURY_TIMELOCK_SECS`
at the moment of proposal. Gives users a public window to
detect a malicious authority and migrate before the swap.

fields.name: max_pool_tvl_usdc
docs: Maximum TVL allowed for a single pool, in USDC base units.
Enforced at `init_pool_vaults` against the pool's max committed
flow = `credit_amount * cycles_total`. A pool is the discrete
unit of trust — capping per-pool bounds blast radius if a
single pool turns adversarial.
`0` means **disabled** (no cap) for back-compat with existing
devnet pools. Mainnet canary plan sets a low value (e.g. $5)
and ramps via `update_protocol_config` as canary data justifies.

fields.name: max_protocol_tvl_usdc
docs: Maximum TVL allowed across all pools combined, in USDC base
units. Enforced at `init_pool_vaults` against
`committed_protocol_tvl_usdc + new_pool_committed`. Caps the
protocol's total exposure during canary roll-out.
`0` means **disabled** (no cap). Mainnet canary plan starts at
a small bound (e.g. $5–50) and ramps in 4 waves per the plan
in `docs/operations/mainnet-canary-plan.md` §7.

fields.name: committed_protocol_tvl_usdc
docs: Running total of committed TVL across active pools.
- Incremented at `init_pool_vaults` by
`credit_amount * cycles_total` of the new pool.
- Decremented at `close_pool` by the same amount when the
pool reaches Completed/Liquidated.
This is "max possible flow" not "current outstanding". The
conservative bound is what canary safety asks for.

fields.name: approved_yield_adapter
docs: Pinned yield-adapter program ID. If non-default, `create_pool`
rejects any `args.yield_adapter` that doesn't match this value.
`Pubkey::default()` (all-zero) **disables** the allowlist —
pools may point at any executable program. This is the devnet
default; mainnet canary sets this to the deployed
`roundfi-yield-kamino` program ID before the first pool is
created, locking the canary's yield-adapter surface.
Mutable via `update_protocol_config` so canary rampup can
rotate (e.g. mock → kamino → kamino-v2). Distinct from
`default_yield_adapter` above, which is informational metadata
that's frozen at init.

fields.name: approved_yield_adapter_locked
docs: One-way kill switch for the adapter allowlist. Mirrors
`treasury_locked` (#122) — once `true`, the
`approved_yield_adapter` Pubkey is permanently frozen:
`update_protocol_config` rejects further changes with
`AdapterAllowlistLocked`. Authority calls
`lock_approved_yield_adapter()` post-canary when confident
the production yield-adapter program ID is final. Idempotent
(calling twice is a no-op, not an error).

fields.name: commit_reveal_required
docs: Gates the legacy single-step `escape_valve_list` path.
When `true`, the legacy ix rejects with `CommitRevealRequired`
and sellers must use `escape_valve_list_commit` +
`escape_valve_list_reveal` (which apply the
`REVEAL_COOLDOWN_SECS` anti-snipe window). When `false`, both
paths coexist and the legacy listing is immediately buyable
(`buyable_after = listed_at`).
Mainnet flip path: authority sets to `true` after the canary
validates the commit-reveal UX. Devnet stays `false` so demo
flows keep their single-step ergonomics. Mutable via
`update_protocol_config`; no lock-flag because flipping back
to permissive is a recoverable operator decision (not a
trust-surface change).

fields.name: lp_share_bps
docs: Share of the post-fee-and-GF residual that routes to LPs /
Anjos de Liquidez. **Authoritative protocol policy** — was
previously caller-controlled via `HarvestYieldArgs.lp_share_bps`,
which let any permissionless cranker rotate the LP/participant
split arbitrarily per call. The harvest_yield handler now
reads this field and ignores the args value (the args field
is retained for SDK back-compat but deprecated).
Default initialized to `DEFAULT_LP_SHARE_BPS` (6_500 = 65%) per
the whitepaper. Mutable via `update_protocol_config` (capped
at MAX_BPS = 10_000). No lock-flag — LP economics may need
adjustment over time as canary-driven calibration progresses.

fields.name: pending_authority
docs: Pending authority rotation. `Pubkey::default()` (all-zero) when
no rotation is queued. Set by `propose_new_authority`, cleared
by `cancel_new_authority` or finalized by `commit_new_authority`.
Mirrors the treasury rotation pattern (#122): authority signs
the propose, anyone can crank the commit after the timelock —
so the rotation eventually completes even if the old authority
key goes offline mid-window.
Use case: bootstrap deployer → Squads multisig vault PDA at
mainnet ceremony time. Once the vault is the authority, ongoing
rotations (Squads-A → Squads-B) flow through the same instructions.

fields.name: pending_authority_eta
docs: Earliest unix-ts at which `commit_new_authority` may execute.
`0` when no rotation is pending. Equals
`now + TREASURY_TIMELOCK_SECS` (7d) at the moment of proposal —
the same window the treasury rotation uses, since authority is
at least as sensitive a surface (authority controls treasury,
fees, pause, allowlists, the whole config).
No `authority_locked` kill-switch counterpart: locking authority
permanently would break future Squads-A → Squads-B rotations
(e.g. if a member key is compromised and the multisig needs to
re-form with new members). Treasury has lock_treasury because
"freeze the fee sink forever" is a coherent end-state; authority
has no equivalent end-state.

fields.name: pending_fee_bps_yield
docs: Pending `fee_bps_yield` change, gated by a 1-day public window.
**Why a timelock on fees:** SEV-024 capped `fee_bps_yield` at
`MAX_FEE_BPS_YIELD = 30%` to bound the blast-radius of an
authority compromise. The W3 audit (Risk #4 + point #6) asked
for a deeper fix: a public window so users can detect a fee
change and opt out via the escape valve before it takes effect.
`0` is NOT a sentinel here (it's a valid value — fee disabled).
`pending_fee_bps_yield_eta == 0` is the "no pending change"
signal; the propose handler sets it to `now + FEE_BPS_YIELD_TIMELOCK_SECS`,
the cancel/commit handlers reset it to 0.
This is the **pilot** for the 6-field timelock pattern documented
in `docs/security/economic-config-governance.md`. The other 5
bps fields (cycle_l1/l2/l3, guarantee_fund_bps, lp_share_bps)
follow the same shape and ship in subsequent PRs.

fields.name: pending_fee_bps_yield_eta
docs: Earliest unix-ts at which `commit_new_fee_bps_yield` may execute.
`0` when no fee change is pending.

fields.name: reputation_program_locked
docs: One-way confirmation that the `reputation_program` slot was
initialised to a real program (i.e. not `Pubkey::default()`).
Mirrors `treasury_locked` / `approved_yield_adapter_locked`,
but with a critical extra invariant: `lock_reputation_program`
**refuses to lock** when `reputation_program == Pubkey::default()`.
Once `true`, the protocol has proven on-chain that it shipped
with reputation wired up — i.e. the "no-reputation" skip paths
in `contribute` / `claim_payout` (which fire when
`reputation_program == default`) can never be reached on this
deployment again, because the field is immutable post-init
(`update_protocol_config.rs` lists it as frozen).
Partner review MEDIUM #2 (2026-06-12). Devnet/test harnesses keep
the `false` default and continue to exercise the skip path (the
bankrun fixtures rely on `reputation_program = default`); mainnet
operators call `lock_reputation_program` immediately after
`initialize_protocol` to guarantee the live deployment can't
silently boot in no-reputation mode.
**Layout note.** Placed at the end of the struct so existing
on-chain ProtocolConfig accounts (devnet, post-`migrate_protocol_config`)
read this field from a zero byte of the forward-compat pad — no
migration needed, no shift of any pre-existing offset.

fields.name: checkpoint
docs: Milestone index 1..=cycles_total. Must be greater than
`member.last_released_checkpoint` (monotonic).

fields.name: cycle
docs: Cycle at which the member defaulted. Must equal `pool.current_cycle`.
Included so the crank caller commits to the exact scenario they
computed off-chain.

types.name: SkipDefaultedPayoutArgs
docs: Permissionless cycle advance for a contemplation cycle whose contemplated
member defaulted **before** their payout slot.
**Why this exists (liveness).** `claim_payout` is the only instruction that
advances `pool.current_cycle`, and it requires the claimant to be the
current slot's member (`slot_index == cycle`) AND `!member.defaulted`. A
member who defaults pre-contemplation is settled (`defaulted = true`) by the
permissionless `settle_default` crank during the grace window — so when
their own contemplation cycle arrives, NO ONE can claim it and the pool
would lock forever (never reaching `Completed` / `close_pool`). This
instruction advances such a cycle WITHOUT disbursing the credit: the
forfeited pot stays in the pool float as surplus (mirrors the L1 Stress Lab
`calote_pre` model, where the defaulter's `received` is 0 and the
undisbursed pot remains in `poolBalance`). It restores the
"No indefinite locks" guarantee in `docs/architecture.md`.
Surfaced by the litesvm L1↔L2 parity slice (`tests/litesvm_parity.spec.ts`).

fields.name: cycle
docs: Must equal `pool.current_cycle` AND the defaulted member's `slot_index`.

fields.name: new_max_pool_tvl_usdc
docs: Per-pool TVL cap, USDC base units. `Some(0)` disables the cap;
`None` leaves the field unchanged. Used by the mainnet canary
rampup — start tight, raise as canary data justifies.

fields.name: new_max_protocol_tvl_usdc
docs: Protocol-wide TVL cap, USDC base units. Same convention as
`new_max_pool_tvl_usdc`.

fields.name: new_approved_yield_adapter
docs: Yield-adapter allowlist pin (item 4.4 of MAINNET_READINESS).
`Some(Pubkey::default())` disables the allowlist (back-compat).
`Some(<program_id>)` requires `create_pool` to match.
`None` leaves the field unchanged.

fields.name: new_commit_reveal_required
docs: Commit-reveal gate (#232). `Some(true)` disables the legacy
single-step `escape_valve_list` path; `Some(false)` re-enables
it. `None` leaves unchanged. Mainnet flips to `Some(true)`
after canary validates the commit-reveal flow.

fields.name: new_lp_share_bps
docs: Yield waterfall LP/participant split (Adevar Labs SEV-003 fix).
`Some(bps)` updates `config.lp_share_bps` — the authoritative
value harvest_yield reads (the args field there is now
deprecated). Capped at MAX_BPS (10_000). `None` leaves unchanged.
